message:
fix(ft-api): multi_margin_loss composes through autograd primitives [bd-x9cr]

multi_margin_loss used to extract values, accumulate the per-sample
sum in plain f64, and rebuild a requires_grad=false leaf —
silently severing autograd for multi-class margin training paths.

Compose through autograd-aware primitives:
   mask_y       = one_hot(target, classes)              [batch, classes]
   mask_others  = 1 - mask_y
   score_y      = sum(input * mask_y, dim=1)            [batch]
   score_y_b    = expand(unsqueeze(score_y, 1), [batch, classes])
   diff         = margin + (input - score_y_b)
   per_elem     = relu(diff) * mask_others              (zero at j == y)
   per_elem_p   = per_elem^p
   per_sample   = sum(per_elem_p, dim=1) / classes
   loss         = mean(per_sample)

The score_y gathering uses (input * mask_y).sum(dim=1) instead of
integer indexing, so the autograd tape carries gradients through
input. one_hot validates target indices internally so we drop the
manual pre-check.

Adds multi_margin_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input regression
test: input=[[0, 3]], target=[0], p=1, margin=1 yields analytical
grad = [-0.5, 0.5]:
  - x[0,1]=3 contributes via diff[0,1] = 1 - 0 + 3 = 4 → relu = 4
  - ∂diff/∂x[0,0] = -1, ∂diff/∂x[0,1] = +1
  - mean(/2) factor on the sum-over-classes (/2) => 1/2 each
  Asserts to 1e-12.

Last severed loss-function in the cluster (sister of ilwr / 4liu /
tr2a).

Closes frankentorch-x9cr. ft-api lib tests: 1154 passing (+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

PyTorch signature: torch.block_diag(*tensors). Used in structured ops and GNNs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:43:19.893738981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:52:26.302493878Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:52:26.302253813Z","close_reason":"Implemented tensor_block_diag with 1-D support, 3 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-wze","title":"Escalate blocked_issues_cache import-path page leak and preserve safe tracker workflow","description":"Follow-up to frankentorch-xsp.

Investigation isolated the integrity regression to the import path: explicit `br sync --import-only` and any command that auto-imports current JSONL into a clean vacuumed DB. The stable signature is blocked_issues_cache churn: old blocked-cache table/index pages become `PRAGMA integrity_check` `never used` pages after import/rebuild.

Safe on a clean vacuumed DB: `br info`, `br doctor`, `br sync --status`, and commands with `--no-auto-import`.

Artifacts:
- artifacts/beads_recovery/FRANKENTORCH_XSP_IMPORT_PATH_REPORT_20260407.md
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_probe_20260407T025948Z/results.json
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_import_focus_20260407T030054Z/results.json
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_repeat_import_20260407T030112Z/results.json
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_sequence_20260407T030136Z/results.json

Next work:
1. Escalate the import-path / blocked-cache page leak upstream in beads_rust or frankensqlite.
2. Preserve a local recovery playbook that warns agents to avoid auto-import on freshly vacuumed recovery DBs until the upstream fix lands.
3. Re-validate the workaround after the upstream fix by repeating the xsp probe matrix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:04:23.139752494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T19:11:33.555177457Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T19:11:33.554917796Z","close_reason":"Filed upstream escalation as beads_rust#224, expanded the archived import-path report with a concrete local recovery playbook, and added a temporary AGENTS.md warning to keep vacuumed recovery DB sessions on --no-auto-import until the fix lands.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-x6h","title":"Normalize remaining rustfmt drift in ft-api and ft-optim","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:55:31.899432848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T19:03:45.885898894Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T19:03:45.885564691Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-x9cr","title":"multi_margin_loss severs autograd; composable through gather/relu/pow/mask","description":"multi_margin_loss at lib.rs:10450 computes per-sample sum_{j != y} relu(margin - input[i,y] + input[i,j])^p / classes, accumulates in plain f64, and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf — silently severing autograd for multi-class margin training paths.

PyTorch's torch.nn.functional.multi_margin_loss IS differentiable (sub-gradient at the relu hinge). Compose through autograd primitives:
  mask_y = one_hot(target, classes)                                  # [batch, classes]
  mask_others = 1 - mask_y
  score_y = sum(input * mask_y, dim=1, keepdim=True)                 # [batch, 1]
  score_y_b = expand to [batch, classes]
  diff = (margin scalar) + input - score_y_b
  per_elem = relu(diff) * mask_others                                # zero at j == y
  per_elem_p = per_elem^p
  loss = mean(sum(per_elem_p, dim=1)) / classes

Sister of the recent loss-severance cluster (ilwr / 4liu / tr2a). Surfaced during /reality-check pass.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Composed multi_margin_loss through one_hot + relu + pow + sum_dim + mean primitives. The mask_others = 1 - one_hot(target) handles the j != y exclusion via element-wise multiplication. score_y is gathered via input * mask_y then summed (instead of integer indexing). The forward shape contract is preserved; existing value tests continue to pass. Added multi_margin_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test (input=[[0,3]], target=[0], p=1, margin=1 → analytical [-0.5, 0.5]). ft-api lib tests: 1154.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:25:03.867756158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:27:34.123425578Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:27:34.120294817Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-xkx","title":"Record session-level functional API coverage in feature parity matrix","description":"Update FEATURE_PARITY.md so the parity matrix and detailed coverage explicitly mention the verified torch.nn.functional-style session API surface: normalize, dropout, embedding, and linear.","notes":"Recorded the verified session-level torch.nn.functional-style API surface in FEATURE_PARITY.md by adding a parity-matrix row and detailed coverage entry for functional_normalize, functional_dropout, functional_embedding, and functional_linear.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:16:07.091943750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-28T05:16:27.688538549Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T05:16:27.687912755Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-xnb","title":"Repair beads SQLite integrity drift after doctor-repair","description":"## Context
`br doctor --repair` recovered ready-state correctness, but it still reports `sqlite.integrity_check: database disk image is malformed: page 17 is never used`. JSONL is canonical and `br ready --json` is already correct, so the remaining work is to rebuild the SQLite side cleanly from JSONL and verify the tracker is healthy.

## Goal
Restore `.beads` to a clean, non-stale state with `br doctor` green except for any known frankensqlite-only sidecar warning.

## Acceptance
- `br ready --json` remains stable
- `br doctor` no longer reports `db.recoverable_anomalies` or stale ready-state drift
- If possible, clear the SQLite malformed-page warning by rebuilding DB from JSONL
- Document any residual unavoidable warning in the close reason","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:43:56.268981040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:45:52.030180722Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:45:52.030002705Z","close_reason":"Snapshotted .beads, rebuilt an isolated DB from canonical JSONL, promoted it over the stale canonical DB, and verified br ready is empty plus br doctor cleared recoverable anomalies; residual warnings are limited to preserved backup artifacts, frankensqlite WAL sidecar behavior, missing sqlite3, and a page-17 integrity warning that persists even after a fresh rebuild","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
